generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component embedded in Building Control Systems (BCS) that receives commands from the Digital Twin to adjust lighting intensity based on occupancy and environmental conditions. Directly modeled within BIM for automated metadata extraction and runtime integration with Eclipse Ditto."
]

instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller component within BCS that adjusts HVAC settings (temperature, humidity) based on real-time sensor data processed by the Digital Twin. Metadata extracted from BIM enables bidirectional communication via Eclipse Ditto."
]

instance EnvironmentalActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic environmental actuator for non-visual health effects (e.g., air quality, UV lighting) controlled through Digital Twin optimization algorithms. Integrated with BIM for consistent device configuration."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-based occupancy sensor deployed across building spaces to detect presence via PIR or ultrasonic technology. Transmits real-time occupancy data via MQTT to Eclipse Hono for Digital Twin processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision IoT sensor measuring temperature and humidity levels in building environments. Data published to Eclipse Hono via MQTT for integration into the Digital Twin's environmental monitoring system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance LightSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Photometric sensor measuring ambient light levels to support adaptive lighting control systems. Data transmitted to Eclipse Hono for correlation with occupancy and visual comfort optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightDataTransmitted
]

instance PermafrostTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized temperature sensor for critical infrastructure areas requiring precise environmental monitoring. Data ingested via Eclipse Hono for predictive maintenance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CriticalEnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance LightDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightSensor
]

instance CriticalEnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PermafrostTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic backbone of the Digital Twin representing geometric, spatial, and functional data of building components. Enriched with DT metadata for automated device discovery and middleware configuration."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance RuntimeMetadataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Extracted from BIM containing device IDs, sensor configurations, and control logic policies. Serves as runtime configuration for middleware platforms and service integration."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance SensorDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data repository containing temperature, occupancy, and light measurements ingested via Eclipse Hono. Used as input for visualization and predictive analytics services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, LightDataTransmitted, CriticalEnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance ControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Embedded in BIM containing lighting control policies, HVAC schedules, and adaptive control algorithms. Executed via Eclipse Ditto for runtime adjustments to physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance VisualizationAssets : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "3D models and interactive interfaces in Unreal Engine 5 representing building components for immersive monitoring and analysis. Used as visualization output for real-time monitoring services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataRepository
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "IoT middleware platform enabling real-time data ingestion and device management for all physical sensors. Standardized MQTT interface connects sensors to the Digital Twin pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runtime configuration platform enabling bidirectional communication between virtual and physical assets. Manages device states and control policies dynamically."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedCommissioningService, ReinforcementLearningService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity 3D visualization environment for immersive monitoring and interactive simulation of building operations. Integrates with sensor data for real-time visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven framework automating extraction of semantics from BIM and generating runtime artifacts (middleware configurations, control logic) via model-to-model transformations."
    DTDFVocab:enables InitializationService, DeploymentService
]

instance BIMToolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Industry-standard BIM software (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) used for modeling physical assets and embedding Digital Twin metadata. Serves as the semantic foundation for all other components."
    DTDFVocab:enables ValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time visualization of building operations through Unreal Engine 5 interface, correlating sensor data with BIM models for immersive monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupancyVisualizationInsight, EnvironmentalComfortInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating reinforcement learning algorithms to optimize energy consumption and visual comfort based on real-time sensor data and occupancy patterns."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendation
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling bidirectional communication between virtual and physical assets for automated device configuration and control logic deployment without manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationAction
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service rendering interactive 3D visualization of building components in Unreal Engine 5, enabling stakeholders to monitor and analyze operational data in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingOperationDashboardInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing sensor data for anomalies to preempt equipment failures and generate maintenance alerts for critical building systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceAlertAction
]

instance ReinforcementLearningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service applying reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust control policies (e.g., lighting schedules) based on real-time operational data and optimization objectives."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPolicyAdjustmentAction
]

instance RuntimeConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling dynamic adjustment of control policies and sensor configurations without full redeployment, supporting continuous optimization of building operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ConfigurationUpdateAction
]

instance StakeholderDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing visual analytics dashboards for Facility Managers to monitor and adjust building operations in real-time based on Digital Twin insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalEfficiencyMetricsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OccupancyVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of occupancy patterns across building spaces, enabling stakeholders to assess utilization and optimize space management."
]

instance EnvironmentalComfortInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, light) to evaluate and optimize occupant comfort and health effects."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven suggestions for adjusting energy consumption patterns based on occupancy and environmental data to achieve operational efficiency."
]

instance VisualComfortOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of lighting systems to maintain optimal visual comfort levels while minimizing energy usage."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeviceConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated deployment of device configurations and control logic to physical systems via bidirectional communication channels."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proactive alerts generated when sensor data indicates potential equipment failures, enabling preventive maintenance actions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlPolicyAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of building control policies (e.g., HVAC schedules, lighting sequences) based on reinforcement learning outputs."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ConfigurationUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Runtime updates to sensor configurations and control parameters without system redeployment, supporting continuous optimization."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalEfficiencyMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive metrics tracking energy consumption trends, occupancy patterns, and environmental comfort indicators for long-term asset management."
]

instance BuildingOperationDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard providing real-time visualization of building operations and key performance indicators for operational monitoring."
]

// C18: Environment/Component
instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BIMModel, SensorDataRepository, VisualizationAssets
    base:isContainedIn BuildingControlSystem
]

instance BuildingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized control infrastructure integrating all physical actuators and sensors within the building. Managed through bidirectional communication with the Digital Twin."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinCore
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized processing unit coordinating all Digital Twin services, enablers, and data flows. Connects physical and virtual domains through standardized interfaces."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingEnvironment
]

// Added missing services and insights
instance InitializationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for initializing the Digital Twin system by loading BIM models, configuring middleware connections, and setting up initial sensor configurations."
]

instance DeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling the deployment of runtime configurations and control logic to middleware platforms and physical systems."
]

instance EvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing the continuous evolution of the Digital Twin by integrating new services, updating control policies, and adapting to changing operational requirements."
]

instance ValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing automated validation checks on BIM models and Digital Twin configurations to ensure semantic consistency and completeness."
]

instance MetadataEnrichmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enriching BIM models with Digital Twin metadata including device configurations, sensor specifications, and control logic policies."
]

instance OccupancyPatternService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing occupancy data patterns to optimize space utilization and predict future occupancy trends for better building management."
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupancyVisualizationInsight
]

// Added missing environment relationship
instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BIMModel, SensorDataRepository, VisualizationAssets, BuildingControlSystem
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinCore
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical interaction occurs via **control commands** generated by the DT and transmitted back to physical actuators and controllers. These commands are derived from simulation, optimization algorithms, or user inputs (e.g., Facility Managers adjusting lighting settings via the DT interface). The framework leverages Eclipse Ditto for runtime configuration and control logic execution, ensuring low-latency, bidirectional communication with physical systems. Actuators execute commands based on the DT’s generated outputs (e.g., adjusting light levels, HVAC settings)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**, with synchronization frequencies determined by sensor data rates (e.g., occupancy sensors updating every 5–30 seconds, environmental sensors updating every 1–10 seconds). Control loops and optimization algorithms (e.g., reinforcement learning in BOREALIS) may operate on slower time scales (e.g., hourly or daily) for adaptive adjustments. The framework supports configurable time scales for different DT services, ensuring alignment with operational requirements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented, modular architecture** with the following components:
   - **BIM Core**: Centralized semantic repository for physical asset data.
   - **Middleware Layer**: Eclipse Hono (data ingestion) and Ditto (runtime control) for bidirectional communication.
   - **Service Layer**: Customizable services (e.g., optimization, visualization) integrated via standardized extension points.
   - **Visualization Layer**: Unreal Engine 5 for interactive 3D monitoring and user interaction.
   The constellation supports **scalable deployment** across heterogeneous infrastructure systems, with clear separation of concerns between data, control, and visualization."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans **multiple lifecycle stages** of the physical asset:
   - **Design phase**: BIM models are enriched with device and sensor metadata for DT deployment.
   - **Construction phase**: Physical sensors and actuators are installed and configured, with metadata synchronized from BIM.
   - **Operation phase**: Real-time data exchange and control logic execution occur via middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto).
   - **Maintenance phase**: DT services enable predictive maintenance and adaptive control adjustments.
   The DT represents the physical asset **as operated**, integrating runtime data to bridge the gap between design and operational realities."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Automated validation**: BIM models are validated for completeness and semantic consistency before deployment.
   - **Real-time synchronization**: Continuous data exchange between physical sensors and the DT minimizes divergence (semantic drift).
   - **ODTE metric**: Measures the alignment between the DT and physical asset, quantifying data accuracy and control effectiveness.
   - **Uncertainty handling**: Runtime adjustments account for sensor noise, latency, and environmental variability in control logic.
   The framework emphasizes **verification and validation (V&V) mechanisms** at design-time (BIM validation) and runtime (ODTE monitoring)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT employs **MQTT-based protocols** over Eclipse Hono for real-time data ingestion from IoT sensors, with low-latency communication ensured via WebSockets or CoAP for critical control signals. Eclipse Ditto provides a **RESTful API** for bidirectional communication between the DT and physical actuators, enabling command execution and status updates. Network architectures are designed for **scalability and fault tolerance**, with middleware components deployed in distributed environments (e.g., cloud or edge computing)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted in a **modular, cloud-native architecture**, with core components (e.g., middleware, visualization) deployed on scalable cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure) or edge devices for low-latency operation. Unreal Engine 5 runs on high-performance GPUs for interactive visualization, while middleware (Hono/Ditto) operates on containerized microservices (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) for elasticity. The deployment supports **hybrid cloud-edge models**, enabling distributed processing of sensor data and control logic."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates horizontally with **external information systems** such as:
   - **Building Management Systems (BMS)**: For centralized control and monitoring of building infrastructure.
   - **Energy Management Systems (EMS)**: To optimize energy usage across multiple buildings or sites.
   - **Facility Management Platforms**: For asset tracking, maintenance scheduling, and compliance reporting.
   Integration is achieved via **standardized APIs** (e.g., OPC UA, REST) and middleware protocols (e.g., MQTT), ensuring interoperability with heterogeneous systems. The framework supports **plug-and-play integration** of third-party services via its modular architecture."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is **primarily with the physical asset owner** (e.g., building owner or facility manager), though middleware providers (e.g., Eclipse Foundation) may manage operational data temporarily during transmission. Privacy considerations include:
   - **Encrypted data transmission**: MQTT and REST APIs use TLS/SSL for secure communication.
   - **Access controls**: Role-based permissions (e.g., Facility Managers vs. DT Engineers) via Ditto’s authentication mechanisms.
   - **Anonymization**: Sensitive data (e.g., occupancy patterns) may be aggregated or anonymized for analytics.
   The framework adheres to **GDPR and industry-specific regulations** (e.g., ISO 55000 for asset management) to ensure compliance."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT follows **industry and open-source standards**:
   - **BIM standards**: IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) for semantic interoperability across AECO tools.
   - **IoT protocols**: MQTT (OASIS standard) for real-time data exchange via Eclipse Hono.
   - **Middleware standards**: Eclipse Ditto’s Thing Model API for device and service modeling.
   - **Visualization standards**: Unreal Engine 5’s USD (Universal Scene Description) for 3D asset interchange.
   The framework leverages **model-driven engineering (MDE) principles** to automate compliance with these standards during DT deployment."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety measures include:
   - **Authentication and authorization**: Role-based access control (RBAC) via Ditto for secure DT interactions.
   - **Data integrity**: End-to-end encryption (TLS/SSL) for all communications between PT and DT.
   - **Isolation**: Containerized deployment (e.g., Docker) to prevent cross-service vulnerabilities.
   - **Safety protocols**: Remote control operations are subject to **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., emergency shutdowns) to prevent accidents. The DT does not execute critical operations without human oversight, adhering to **ISO 27001** for information security and **ISO 45001** for occupational safety. Audit logs track all DT interactions for compliance and forensics."
]
